nodes.context: Indigenous Latino American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, member of People Nation.
Gang alliance formed in Pontiac Correctional Center.
Gang that operated in Back of the Yards community, Chicago.
Chicago street gang, rival of Latin Counts.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1978, the Counts were invited to join the People Nation
edges.context: Latin Counts became a member of the People Nation alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: inspired by the Polish Counts gang that operated in the nearby Back of the Yards community
edges.context: Latin Counts named themselves after the Polish Counts gang.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Counts came into conflict with other gangs in the Pilsen area such as the Latin Kings
edges.context: Latin Counts were in conflict with Latin Kings in the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Counts came into conflict with other gangs in the Pilsen area such as the Latin Kings , the Satan Disciples and Ambrose.
edges.context: Latin Counts were in conflict with Satan Disciples in the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Counts came into conflict with other gangs in the Pilsen area such as the Latin Kings , the Satan Disciples and Ambrose.
edges.context: Latin Counts were in conflict with Ambrose in the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1970, a faction broke off from the gang to be named the Bishops, who retained good relations with the Counts due to their sharing of the same bloodlines.
edges.context: Bishops are a splinter faction of Latin Counts with good relations.

edges.evidence_quote: This enabled the Counts to enter a truce with the allied Latin Kings, but street-level conflict continued between the two gangs until the 1980s.
edges.context: Latin Counts had a truce with Latin Kings but conflict continued until 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1991, a full-scale shooting war erupted with the Latin Kings over turf in Cicero that pitted all Counts and Kings against each other nationwide.
edges.context: Full-scale war between Latin Counts and Latin Kings in 1991.
